title: He built a toy to teach math. It sold 500 MILLION #Shorts
desc: In 1974 a Hungarian design professor named Erno Rubik built a puzzle out of 27 wooden blocks to teach his students how to think in three dimensions. He wasn't chasing money, and after scrambling his own cube it took him about a month to solve it. Once he licensed it as the Rubik's Cube in 1980 it sold over 100 million units in about three years and around 500 million to date, the best-selling toy in history, and the royalties made the professor wealthy.
